## Per-adversarial-category strict F1

| reference_no_value | empty 100% |
| zero_pii_control | empty 100% |

## Held-out (50 Wikipedia carriers + injected PII)

_Ground truth = injected PII only; precision is structurally biased downward because predictors correctly find pre-existing Wikipedia PII that isn't in the injection-only ground truth. **Recall on injected PII** is the meaningful metric on this set._

| Predictor | Recall on injected | Strict F1 | n FP | n injected |
|-----------|---------------------|-----------|------|------------|
| presidio | 63.3% | 0.187 | 560 | 109 |
<!-- AEGIS_TABLE_END -->

## Setup

```
make setup
```

Requires Python 3.11+. Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and fill in any keys you
have — eval gracefully skips baselines whose keys are missing.

## Re-running the eval

```bash
# Run a single baseline against the eval suite (writes eval/results/<name>/)
python -m eval.run_baseline presidio
python -m eval.run_baseline openai          # needs OPENAI_API_KEY
python -m eval.run_baseline gemini          # needs GOOGLE_API_KEY
python -m eval.run_baseline qwen-base       # local; slow on CPU

# Cross-LLM-judge calibration (requires both API keys)
python -m eval.calibration --predictor presidio --sample 50

# Update the README's auto-generated block from current results
python -m eval.harness --inject README.md
```

## Layout

- [data/](data/) — dataset construction, schema, ai4privacy downloader, eval-set builders
- [train/](train/) — Colab-runnable QLoRA training script (Qwen 2.5 7B + Unsloth)
- [inference/](inference/) — model loader, shared `SYSTEM_PROMPT`
- [baselines/](baselines/) — GPT-4o-mini, Gemini Flash, base Qwen, Presidio (uniform `Predictor` interface)
- [eval/](eval/) — metrics, runner, perf, calibration, harness — **built before training**
- [cli/](cli/) — `aegis detect`, `aegis eval`, `aegis compare` (forthcoming)

## Calibration approach

No human gold standard. Two independent LLM judges (GPT-4o + Gemini 2.5 Pro)
score the same 50 adversarial outputs against gold using a calibrated 1-5
rubric; reported `r` is the Pearson correlation between them. Target r ≥ 0.6;
below that, the rubric in [eval/calibration.py](eval/calibration.py) is iterated
before any judge-based number is reported.

This is a deliberate tradeoff for the portfolio context — it's documented
honestly here rather than hidden.

## Build order

1. ✅ Project skeleton
2. ✅ Data pipeline (privacy_mask → char-offset spans, train/val/test split)
3. ✅ Held-out (Wikipedia + injection, 50) + adversarial (50) eval sets
4. ✅ Eval harness (metrics, runner, perf, calibration, comparison-table renderer)
5. ⏳ Training script (Colab)
6. ⏳ Training run on Colab A100
7. ⏳ Re-run full eval; delta from baselines = headline result
8. ⏳ Iterate if F1 < GPT-4o-mini
9. ⏳ CLI, tests (60% coverage on non-trivial paths), Dockerfile

## What this README will become

Once the comparison block above is fully populated (Presidio ✓, GPT-4o-mini ⏳,
Gemini Flash ⏳, base Qwen ⏳, Aegis ⏳), this README is the headline artifact:
real numbers, real methodology, real limitations.
